criteria_checked.note: pre-tool-guard.sh:55 (json_get 'permission_mode'), :136 active-gate, :140-145 precedence install->publish->audit-force->schema-push->migrate, :152 exact bypassPermissions, :155 log line, :156-157 systemMessage, :158 exit 0 — all match the plan's quoted strings.

dmc_codex_common.py:46-52 has no PERMISSION_MODE_KEYS; grep permission_mode adapters/ = 0; _FLOORS:296-300 is the sole 'ask' tuple (all others 'deny'), so classify_bash_floors returning 'ask' uniquely identifies Block C.
dmc-codex-pretooluse.py:46-50 — verdict=='deny' -> pretool_deny (exits) precedes the verdict=='ask' branch; stand-down is structurally unreachable for any deny command.
_FLOORS:300 ask row scope='active'; classify_bash_floors:311-312 skips it when mode!='active' -> new bypass path reachable only in active mode, mirroring pre-tool-guard.sh:136.
_m65common.sh:55 c_bash emits no permission_mode; test-codex-shims.sh:333 D9 (npm install) stays ask==ask; adding inert-if-absent bypass-awareness changes no existing row.

run_m65_suite (bin/dmc:280) is bash "$s"||rc=1, exit-code only; the sole code-pinned count is legacy PINNED_BASELINE 802/3/3 (dmc-legacy-selftest.py:118) over dmc-v0.* files only (:208). The '143 assertions' figure is descriptive prose, not a pin — section F breaks nothing.

AGENTS.md:183,186 list both .py as enforcement (path+class, no content field); INSTALL_MANIFEST.md:151-152 by name; dmc-install.sh:44 CODEX_ADAPTERS + :370 ship_file = :251 cp byte-copy (no embedded payload). Neutrality (agents-md/--emit-manifest diffs EMPTY) is a blocking verification AC; drift => HALT + follow-up scope, never in-lock edit.

DEFAULT_PROTECTED (dmc-v0.2.6-gate-check-runner.sh:22-31) excludes adapters/ and adapters/codex, so editing the codex .py files does not trip G4; no DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override needed (non-degrading FLAG only).

_ci_get (dmc_codex_common.py:85-98) lowercases keys only and returns str(v) unchanged, so == 'bypassPermissions' is byte-exact; absent => '' => falls through to ask (inert-if-absent). Value-blind log line never exercises redact().

Stand-down sits at the ask branch, reached only after classify_bash_floors returns deny and exits (F3); Block C is the sole ask-scoped floor; section F F4/F5 assert deny-under-bypass and the grep-AC proves the bypassPermissions token never appears on a deny path.

Log line carries class + UTC timestamp only, matched by ^[0-9TZ:-]+ ask-tier-standdown class=[a-z-]+$. ADVISORY (1): F-PAR1 must apply ONE shared systemMessage extractor to both adapters; the message value is quote-free/backslash-free ASCII so a single s/.*"systemMessage":"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/ extraction is unambiguous across Claude's printf envelope and Codex's compact json.dumps envelope and yields byte-equal text.

scope.lock == exactly the 4 changed paths (both .py, test-codex-shims.sh, docs/MILESTONES.md), all staged (G2), landmark_authorized on the two .py rows, no override; records/verification/evidence scope-exempt; commits LOCAL on claude/dmc-fable-core, .codex/config.toml unstaged, no push.

ENDORSE in-suite section F: it is a cross-adapter parity property, the claude_run+codex_run harness already lives in test-codex-shims.sh, and only that suite is CI-covered (dmc-ci.yml:172-173 blocking selftest m65-suite); the Claude sibling tests/install/test-ask-tier-bypass.sh:2 is NOT CI-wired, so a standalone codex test would leave the mirror CI-uncovered.

ENDORSE top-level-only permission_mode reader — mirrors json_get 'permission_mode' (a top-level field); a tool_input-then-top superset would risk a cross-adapter divergence and is unnecessary given inert-if-absent fails closed to ask.

ACCEPTABLE (advisory, not a blocker): including permissionMode is consistent with the module's superset house rule, and _ci_get case-insensitivity does NOT bridge snake<->camel so an explicit key is the only match path. ADVISORY (2): the PERMISSION_MODE_KEYS comment should mark snake permission_mode = documented parity key and camel = defensive-only, and the parity rows must exercise the snake key so cross-adapter parity is machine-checked on the real field (the plan's cbp builder uses snake — good).

ENDORSE (mandatory): the v1.1.5 MILESTONES entry must carry the honest 'live Codex permission_mode delivery turn-free-unprovable / inert-if-absent' line at v1.1.1-equal prominence; no enforcement-parity claim added (Option-A ADVISORY posture, no-masking discipline).

F-PAR1 as specified is testable: it compares EXTRACTED systemMessage strings, not raw envelope bytes. Message text and class values are all ASCII with no chars requiring JSON escaping, so Claude's json_string and Codex's json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False) emit byte-identical inner text; extraction well-defined (folds ADVISORY 1).

Lock == exactly 4 changed paths, no defensive rows; AGENTS.md / INSTALL_MANIFEST.md / _m65common.sh all Allowed-to-Edit NO with HALT-on-drift; G2 staging-obligation (v1.1.4 learning) satisfied by all-staged requirement.

Symmetric: Claude Block C is active-only ([ "$DMC_MODE" = active ]) so passive never reaches it (no ask/standdown/log); Codex classify_bash_floors skips the active-scoped ask row in passive -> (None,None) -> pretool_allow (no envelope, no log). v1.1.1 sibling case 7 already locks the Claude passive behavior. ADVISORY (3): F7 must use a FRESH passive sandbox so 'no log file' is a clean assertion.

T001 (:136) does NOT instruct the executor to run python3 -c — it explicitly delegates py_compile to the suite/verifier and steers away from raw -c. The committed suite's own internal python3 -c (C3, test-codex-shims.sh:308) runs inside bash <file>, not in the executor's tool-call string, so the write-radius classifier sees only 'bash tests/fixtures/m6.5/test-codex-shims.sh' (no redirect/-c/sh -c).
